messages.content: Hey everyone! Random question—what was your absolute favorite cartoon as a kid? I feel like I could talk about cartoons forever 😂

Omg, this is such a good question 😂 I was obsessed with Sailor Moon when I was little! I used to pretend I was fighting evil by moonlight in my backyard lol. Anyone else have a show they watched on repeat?

Haha, Eve, I love that! I was totally into Pokémon. I’d wake up super early just to catch it before school (and probably annoyed my parents with all the theme song singing 😂). I still know all the words! Anyone else have those shows you’d never miss?

Yesss, Charlie! Saturday mornings were sacred for cartoons 😂 Besides Sailor Moon, I was also really into Dexter’s Laboratory and Powerpuff Girls. I swear Cartoon Network was peak TV back then. Did anyone else ever try to invent stuff like Dexter, or was that just me?

Haha, I totally get the Dexter’s Lab thing! I definitely tried to build “secret lairs” out of cardboard boxes and blankets. As for my favorite, I was all about Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles. I wanted to be Michelangelo so bad 😂 Pizza just tasted better after watching those guys fight crime! Anyone else have a cartoon that made you wanna eat (or do) something weird?

Lol yes, Bob! I definitely went through a phase where I only wanted to eat ramen because of anime characters slurping it up on TV. And don’t even get me started on Scooby-Doo—I swear those giant sandwiches Shaggy and Scoob made always looked SO good. Did anyone else ever try to make the weirdest food combos after seeing them in cartoons?

Scooby-Doo sandwiches were the *dream* 😂 I tried stacking like five things together once and it was just a disaster, but hey, gotta try! Also, shoutout to Looney Tunes for making carrots seem way cooler than they actually are. Bugs Bunny basically had me thinking carrots were the ultimate snack. Anyone else have a cartoon food you wanted to try?

Omg yes, cartoon food just always looked so good for some reason! I used to beg my mom to make me “krabby patties” after watching SpongeBob 😂 She would just give me a regular burger but I’d be like, “No, it needs the secret formula!” Also, I was obsessed with Kim Possible—her nachos always looked amazing! Did anyone else want a communicator like hers?

Haha, Diana, I totally wanted a Kim Possible-style communicator! I used to pretend my old flip phone was a spy gadget and send “missions” to my friends 😂 And honestly, Krabby Patties made burgers seem SO much cooler than they actually were. Anyone else ever try to make their own cartoon inventions or gadgets? Or was I just a really nerdy kid?

Not nerdy at all, Bob! I totally used to tape two walkie-talkies together and pretend it was some top-secret gadget like Kim Possible’s communicator 😂 Honestly, cartoons made everything seem way cooler. I also remember wanting a magical remote like in Fairly OddParents to just pause or fast-forward boring parts of my day. Imagine having that power during homework!

Eve, oh my gosh, I totally forgot about Fairly OddParents! I wanted fairy godparents SO badly—imagine wishing your way out of every school project 😂 And yeah, those magical remotes… if only! Did anyone ever try to turn random objects into “magic” stuff because of cartoons? I definitely waved around hairbrushes pretending they were wands like in Totally Spies or something 😆

Haha, Diana, I totally did the same thing! I had this old TV remote I’d pretend was some kind of gadget or magic wand—would just point it at stuff and hope for the best 😂 Anyone else ever make up their own cartoon characters or pretend to “join” the cast on adventures? I used to daydream about being the “sixth Powerpuff Girl” or something wild like that!

Charlie, YES! I definitely made up my own characters and tried to “join” the cartoons in my head 😅 I even drew my own Sailor Scout persona once and gave her a whole backstory (with sparkles, obviously). I’d always imagine jumping into the TV to help solve mysteries with the Scooby gang or fight villains with the Powerpuff Girls. Kid logic is the best—everything just felt possible! Anyone else ever give themselves superpowers after binge-watching cartoons?

Eve, love that! I definitely gave myself superpowers after watching X-Men—I’d just run around the house pretending I could control the weather like Storm 😂 Also, Saturday morning cartoons just hit different with a big bowl of cereal. I swear, something about eating Froot Loops in pajamas made those shows even more magical. Did anyone else have a go-to cereal for cartoon mornings?

Oh totally, Charlie! For me it was Cinnamon Toast Crunch—something about the sugar rush just made cartoons even funnier 😂 Saturday mornings were basically a ritual: cereal, blanket fort, and whatever cartoons were on. I feel like cartoons and cereal are the ultimate combo. Anyone else have a cartoon they HAD to watch every weekend, no matter what?

For me it was definitely Pokémon—like, if I missed an episode, my whole day felt off 😅 I’d grab a bowl of Cap’n Crunch and basically glue myself to the TV until it was over. There was just something about those weekend mornings that felt like pure magic. Did anyone else get super invested in trying to “catch ’em all”—either in the games or just pretending?

Oh man, Pokémon was a must-watch for me too! I had the cards (even though I barely understood how to play lol) and I’d make my own little “Pokéballs” out of paper to act out battles. There was always that one cartoon you just couldn’t miss—I was also big on Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles for a while. I’d run around pretending I had nunchucks (thankfully, just rolled-up socks 😂). Anyone else have a phase where they

Haha, Charlie, I totally had phases! I remember being obsessed with The Powerpuff Girls one month, then totally switching over to Hey Arnold! or Dexter’s Laboratory the next. I was always picking up little catchphrases and trying to use them at school (my teachers were not amused when I’d shout “Sugar, spice, and everything nice!” 😂). Did anyone else go through those cartoon-themed phases where your whole personality revolved around one show?

Absolutely, Diana! I definitely had those phases too—like, one month everything was about Rugrats and I’d insist on wearing pigtails like Angelica, and then suddenly I’d be all about Recess and try to act like I was as cool as Spinelli (spoiler: I was not 😂). It’s wild how a cartoon could take over your whole vibe for a while! Did anyone actually try to recreate any of the snacks or foods from their favorite shows? I remember begging

Haha, Eve, totally! I used to BEG my parents to let me try making a “Krabby Patty” after watching Spongebob. I think I just put random stuff on a burger and called it “secret formula” 😂 I also remember being obsessed with the pizza from Nin

I swear, something about eating Froot Loops in pajamas made those shows even more magical. Did anyone else have a go-to cereal for cartoon mornings?
